chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الدُّعَاءِ لِلْكَرْبِ وَ الْهَمِّ وَ الْحُزْنِ وَ الْخَوْفِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 55 – The supplication for the distress, and the worries, and the grief, and the fear

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/420)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/420)
text: أَنَّ الَّذِي دَعَا بِهِ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَلَى دَاوُدَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ حِينَ قَتَلَ الْمُعَلَّى بْنَ خُنَيْسٍ وَ أَخَذَ مَالَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِنُورِكَ الَّذِي لَا يُطْفَى وَ بِعَزَائِمِكَ الَّتِي لَا تُخْفَى وَ بِعِزِّكَ الَّذِي لَا يَنْقَضِي وَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ الَّتِي لَا تُحْصَى وَ بِسُلْطَانِكَ الَّذِي كَفَفْتَ بِهِ فِرْعَوْنَ عَنْ مُوسَى ( عليه السلام ) .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Abu Ismail Al Sarraj, from Muawiya Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that the supplication with which Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> supplicated against Dawood Bin Ali when he killed Al-Moalla Bin Khunays and seized the wealth of Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was: ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I<sup>asws</sup> ask You<sup>azwj</sup> by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Light Which cannot be extinguished, and by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Determination which is not hidden, and by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Might which cannot expire, and by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Favours which cannot be counted, and by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Authority which suffice Musa<sup>as</sup> from Pharaoh<sup>la</sup>’’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Isma’Il from abu Isma’Il al-Sarraj from Mu’awiyah ibn ‘Ammar from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The supplication of abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) against Dawud ibn Ali when he murdered Mu’alla ibn Khunays and seized the property of abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) was this supplication: ‘O Lord, I plead before You through Your light that does not extinguish, through Your determination that is not hidden, through Your majesty that never ends, through Your bounties that are beyond enumeration and through Your authority with which You prevented the Pharaoh from harming Moses.’”
